subject: Geography
title: Everest: The Roof of the World
description: A short lesson to help you apply Everest: The Roof of the World.

hook.text: At 8,849 meters, Everest is the highest point on Earth. Over 300 people have died climbing it.

buildup.text: Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay reached the summit in 1953, making history.

discovery.text: The 'death zone' above 8,000 meters has so little oxygen that the body begins shutting down.

twist.text: Everest is so crowded now that climbers queue in line near the summit.

climax.text: The mountain grows about 4 mm per year as India keeps pushing into Asia.

punchline.text: The tallest peak on Earth is still getting taller.

quiz.question: Who first reached the summit of Everest?
